checks.finding: Field syntax, column-0/whole-line/value-agnostic deletion mechanism, and the column-0-vs-indented boundary are given with exact worked examples (Field Definitions; Edge Cases sed reference; AC-002/TEST-002(c) non-matching examples). No undefined term found that would let implementers diverge.

Non-goals are consistent with Goals/AC-001-005, which scope the change strictly to task-stage deletion. Security Boundaries' disclosed removal consequence does not conflict with Non-goals leaving check-task-state's Done-time validation unchanged. No AC-vs-AC or AC-vs-non-goal conflict found.

Round-3 edit closes the round-2 gap: AC-001/TEST-001 add the MULTI-OCCURRENCE fixture (T-001/T-002). Corpus now covers add, present-at-freeze-then-edited, multi-occurrence, three tamper sub-cases including the column-0 negative control, CRLF, and no-trailing-newline; every requirements.md Edge Case maps to a TEST-00x row. Non-blocking note: no dedicated row for 'previously recorded line later removed' — same stateless per-line-deletion code path already exercised, not a distinct ambiguous decision.

Both listed Assumptions are explicitly grounded as pre-existing repo facts rather than open guesses. Open Questions is explicitly 'None,' with the deletion-vs-masking choice justified by Problems #2 and enforcement-point scoping justified by Non-goals.

In scope, not skipped. The second-human-approval workflow this feature unblocks is verified end-to-end by AC-001/TEST-001. The human-copy protected-path boundary (hook guard R-10) has an explicit acceptance criterion and gate-phase test, AC-005/TEST-005, requiring recorded evidence that live SHA-256 equals the staged human-copy MANIFEST hash.

Exact mask semantics, bounded scope, edge cases each mapped to a test id, and a stated protected-file application path (human-copy + AC-005) leave no product behavior for a downstream reviewer to invent.
